items.correct: Information the performer senses from their own body during/after movement.
items.why: Students reverse the meaning. Intrinsic = from WITHIN (kinesthetic awareness, feel); extrinsic = from OUTSIDE (coach feedback, video, statistics). Experienced performers rely more on intrinsic feedback.

items.q: Is a tennis serve a closed or open skill?
items.correct: Closed - performed in a predictable, self-paced environment.
items.misconception: Open - because tennis is a fast-paced sport.
items.why: Students judge by the sport, not the moment. The serve happens at the server's pace with a stationary ball - closed. The return of serve is open. Skill classification depends on the SPECIFIC action's environment.

items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.why: Students confuse knee with hip. Both are in the leg, but the hip is ball-and-socket (multi-directional); the knee is a hinge (mostly flexion-extension). Ball-and-socket joints have more range.

items.q: The autonomous stage of learning is characterised by:
items.correct: Skill execution with minimal conscious attention
items.why: It's Skill execution with minimal conscious attention. “Frequent errors” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Feedback about outcome of performance. “Knowledge of biomechanics” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If a beginner makes 30% errors and an expert 5%, percentage point improvement is?
items.why: The real figure is 25. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
